reason: The remediation only edits /etc/security/pwquality.conf (or its .d includes) to set the maxclassrepeat parameter, a PAM password quality setting. Changing this does not affect service start‑up, boot configuration, or network interfaces, and it will not lock out existing users—only future password changes may be rejected if they violate the new rule.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Existing passwords may not meet the new requirement, causing password change failures; Incorrect syntax could render the configuration file unparsable, potentially disabling pwquality checks
